health_zone_table.basis: SitRep57 Table 2 (2026-07-10) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25132, media 25133, pdf sha256 141c9539a4cc0a4a2c31f6972280e4f4251ecedc4aa3e1bf5f7123b53ecb6d05, 13 pages, 1409453 bytes), byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian source-review receipt. National TOTAL 1873/672 EXCLUDES the starred Haut-Uele (Wamba 1/1) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani 2/2, Mangobo 1/0, Lubunga 1/0) rows, which INSP marks '* non comptabilise car deja inclus dans les cas de la ZS de Niania'.

reconciliation.note: Named health-zone rows sum to 1856 confirmed / 672 confirmed deaths. Table 1 counted province totals are Ituri 1705/577, Nord-Kivu 165/94, Sud-Kivu 3/1 = national 1873/672. The Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified is retained as the explicit unventilated row. Nord-Kivu's Table 1 total (165/94) equals the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows, so there is no Nord-Kivu ventilation gap; the derived unallocated residual is 17 confirmed / 0 deaths (Ituri only). Haut-Uele (Wamba, 1/1) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani 2/2, Mangobo 1/0, Lubunga 1/0) are listed by INSP for the first time but are EXCLUDED from the national total this cycle (starred, epidemiologically attributed to the Nia-Nia/PK51 epicentre). Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days with no new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), holding in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 177 analyzed / 42 positive (23.7%); Nord-Kivu 54 analyzed / 1 positive (1.9%); Sud-Kivu 5 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 43 positives over 231 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep57 Tableau 7: 763 in isolation at end of day (259 confirmed / 504 suspected) at 95.1% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu 135.2% saturation, Ituri 88%, Sud-Kivu 53.3%. Exits over 24h (126 total): 18 deaths (all Ituri), 92 non-cases, 6 recoveries (all Ituri), 9 escapes (all Ituri), 1 transfer to HGR (NK); 110 admissions across the three provinces.
Recovered advanced to 306 DRC cumulative (+6). J-1 bed count is 779; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 763 (259 + 504).
highlights: 43 new confirmed cases and 24 deaths (62.5% community) were reported for 2026-07-10, in Ituri (42 cases, 24 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (1 case). Confirmed cases rose to 1873 and deaths to 672; the global CFR is 35.9%. Six Ituri patients recovered after negative control tests.

Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) are listed by INSP for the first time this cycle, epidemiologically attributed to the Nia-Nia/PK51 epicentre via contacts and population movement toward Kisangani and Haut-Uele; they are EXCLUDED from the national total (1873) and shown as starred annex rows.

Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 135.2% and global bed occupancy is 95.1% (763 in isolation, 259 confirmed / 504 suspected). Extension of the Bunia HGR ETC and Kingoze IDP-camp transit centre were inaugurated; 200 secure blood bags were received in Bunia from Kinshasa.

Ituri concentrates the epidemic at 1705 confirmed / 577 deaths, CFR 33.8%. Bunia (503), Rwampara (384), Mongbwalu (329), Nizi (110) and Nyankunde (97) remain the most affected zones; the epidemic is currently progressing fastest in Nizi.
Nord-Kivu totals 165 confirmed / 94 deaths, CFR 57.0%, carried by Katwa (67.2%), Beni (66.7%) and Butembo (46.5%); Katwa (67), Butembo (43) and Beni (30) lead notification.

Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days without a new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), holding in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration; 24 patients remain in isolation.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 3 (10 July): 1497 new alerts + 19 carried over = 1516 total. Investigated 1285 (84.8%). Validated suspects 299 = 208 living + 91 deaths, concordant with the banner suspected-cases-of-day 299 (91 deaths under investigation for possible reclassification). Per-province validated-death split (Ituri 65 / Nord-Kivu 26 / Sud-Kivu 0) is derived so the province rows reconcile to the 299 total and the 208 living total.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 135.2%; national isolation occupancy is 95.1% (763 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 71.7% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert investigation in Ituri (64.8% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu reports a laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis (5.6% positivity) and, in its logistics subset, no functional ambulance or hearse; Mutwanga and Kasindi-Lubiriha flag insufficient IPC inputs and swabs for community-death alerts.
challenges.response: Clear the laboratory backlog, restore transport/EDS logistics, and resupply IPC inputs and swabs to the affected Nord-Kivu health zones.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (10 July): 13760 under follow-up, 9862 seen, 71.7%. Ituri 68.1% (11480/7817), Nord-Kivu 89.7% (2280/2045). Sud-Kivu contact follow-up closed (all listed contacts completed). Tableau 5: 692 new contacts listed (Ituri 543, Nord-Kivu 149), 12 became suspect (Ituri 8, Nord-Kivu 4), 181 completed 21-day follow-up (Ituri 69, Nord-Kivu 112).

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep57 Tableau 7 end-of-day census 763 at 95.1% occupancy (Ituri 543 @88%, Nord-Kivu 196 @135.2%, Sud-Kivu 24 @53.3%). Census 763 = 259 confirmed + 504 suspected; province rows 543+196+24=763 concordant. J-1 bed count 779. Exits (126 total): 18 deaths (all Ituri), 92 non-cases (Ituri 28, NK 64), 6 recoveries (all Ituri), 9 escapes (all Ituri), 1 transfer to HGR (NK); 110 admissions (Ituri 69, NK 36, SK 5).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones, and validate the newly listed Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) detections, epidemiologically attributed to Nia-Nia/PK51 and currently excluded from the national total, before any count-bearing use.

Relieve isolation load at 95.1% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 135.2% saturation (57.0% provincial CFR); accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Bunia ETC extension and Kingoze transit centre inaugurated).
Improve contact follow-up (71.7% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 64.8%, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis.
Sustain residual epidemiological surveillance in Sud-Kivu (42+ days with no new confirmed case) toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration, while resolving the 24 patients still in isolation.

preserved_conflicts: SitRep57 national headline is DRC-only (1873 confirmed / 672 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.

Haut-Uele (Wamba 1/1) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani 2/2, Mangobo 1/0, Lubunga 1/0) appear in Table 2 for the first time but are STARRED ('non comptabilise car deja inclus dans les cas de la ZS de Niania') and EXCLUDED from the national total of 1873/672. They are carried as annex rows and on the epidemiological-attribution note, never summed into named-zone reconciliation.

Nord-Kivu Table 1 total (165/94) equals the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows this cycle, so there is no Nord-Kivu ventilation gap. The only unallocated residual is the 17 Ituri confirmed cases in zones not yet identified (0 deaths), retained as an explicit unventilated row.
Suspected cases of the day total 299 = 208 living + 91 deaths (Tableau 3), concordant with the banner. The 91 community deaths are under investigation for possible reclassification.
Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days with no new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration.

review_notes: SitRep #057 (report date 2026-07-10; INSP post 25132; media 25133; WP publish 2026-07-12T11:10:28Z). PDF sha256 141c9539a4cc0a4a2c31f6972280e4f4251ecedc4aa3e1bf5f7123b53ecb6d05, 13 pages, 1409453 bytes, byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian source-review receipt.

DRC 1873 confirmed / 672 deaths / CFR 35.9% / 306 recovered; +43 confirmed / +24 deaths in 24h (Ituri 42/24, Nord-Kivu 1). Isolation census 763 = 259 confirmed + 504 suspected at 95.1% occupancy. Contact follow-up 71.7%.

Haut-Uele and Tshopo listed for the first time but starred and EXCLUDED from the 1873 total (epi-attributed to Nia-Nia/PK51); named-zone set is unchanged from SR56. Country-scope unallocated is 37 (17 Ituri + 20 Uganda). Sud-Kivu holds 42+ days with no new case.
